title: TypeScript Data Types
content:
### Primitive Types

TypeScript has several primitive data types.

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|--------|
| `string` | Textual data | `"hello"` |
| `number` | Integers and floats | `42`, `3.14` |
| `boolean` | True or false | `true`, `false` |
| `null` | Null value | `null` |
| `undefined` | Undefined value | `undefined` |
| `symbol` | Unique symbols | `Symbol("id")` |
| `bigint` | Large integers | `9007199254740991n` |

### Special Types

| Type | Description |
|------|------------|
| `any` | Disables type checking |
| `unknown` | Type-safe version of any |
| `never` | Never occurs |
| `void` | No return value |

### Object Types

```typescript
// Object type
let person: { name: string; age: number } = {
  name: "Alice",
  age: 30
};

// Array type
let numbers: number[] = [1, 2, 3];
let names: Array<string> = ["Alice", "Bob"];

// Tuple type
let pair: [string, number] = ["Alice", 30];
```

### Type Checking

```typescript
let value: string = "Hello";
console.log(typeof value); // "string"
```

> **Key Point:** TypeScript infers types from values, so explicit annotations are optional but recommended.

quiz.question: Which TypeScript type represents a large integer beyond the safe integer range?
What type should you use to disable type checking for a variable?
